approximate_dynamic_robustness.note: Primary decomposition uses the exact identity-preserving baseline; the approximate dynamic path is retained only as a secondary robustness check.

h0.explanation: Headline pass-through and crowd-out ratios are out of scope in the current release because the repo has not resolved a dimensionally coherent first-stage denominator gate for raw-unit treatment responses.

h4.explanation: Headline pass-through and crowd-out ratios are out of scope in the current release because the repo has not resolved a dimensionally coherent first-stage denominator gate for raw-unit treatment responses.

h8.explanation: Headline pass-through and crowd-out ratios are out of scope in the current release because the repo has not resolved a dimensionally coherent first-stage denominator gate for raw-unit treatment responses.

headline_question: When unexpected bank-only TDC rises, how do total deposits and the non-TDC deposit component respond?
headline_answer: Current run is informative as a deposit-response readout, but it does not yet support a clean pass-through-versus-crowd-out conclusion or broad mechanism attribution.
mechanism_caveat: Structural proxies remain cross-checks on the residual, not standalone proof of mechanism; creator-lane evidence is surfaced separately in counterpart_channel_scorecard.json, and bank_credit_private_qoq remains only a coarse legacy creator proxy.

sample_policy.headline_rule: Keep the frozen headline sample and publish flagged-window trimming as a robustness check, not as a replacement estimand.

flagged_window_robustness.note: Flagged-window trims materially change the headline h0/h4 sign pattern.

proxy_coverage_context.release_caveat: The current public proxy bundle spans a limited set of channel families and still does not exhaust the bank-only non-TDC residual.

takeaways: This scorecard reframes the non-TDC question around counterpart creator channels rather than deposit types.

First-wave creator outcomes currently present: agency_gse_backed_securities_bank_qoq, auto_loans_qoq, closed_end_residential_loans_ex_chargeoffs_qoq, closed_end_residential_loans_qoq, commercial_industrial_loans_ex_chargeoffs_qoq, commercial_industrial_loans_qoq, construction_land_development_loans_qoq, consumer_loans_ex_chargeoffs_qoq, consumer_loans_qoq, corporate_foreign_bonds_bank_qoq, cre_multifamily_loans_qoq, cre_nonfarm_nonresidential_loans_qoq, credit_card_revolving_loans_ex_chargeoffs_qoq, credit_card_revolving_loans_qoq, heloc_loans_qoq, loans_for_purchasing_or_carrying_securities_qoq, loans_to_commercial_banks_qoq, loans_to_nondepository_financial_institutions_qoq, municipal_securities_bank_qoq, other_consumer_loans_ex_chargeoffs_qoq, other_consumer_loans_qoq, treasury_securities_bank_qoq.
Charge-off-adjusted creator lanes add back an approximate quarterly destruction flow using lagged balances and official annualized charge-off rates.
Intermediary, depository, and securities-purpose lending channels are now separated from the core domestic nonfinancial creator lanes.
The first bank asset-purchase creator lane is now live via quarterly Z.1 holdings for Treasury, agency/GSE-backed, municipal, and corporate/foreign bond assets.
Asset-purchase creator signals are now paired with raw TGA, reserves, and combined central-bank plumbing responses so positive securities accumulation is not interpreted without Treasury/Fed context.
The first destroyer / escape block is now live through sign-normalized MMF, repo, ON RRP, direct Treasury / bill, and currency reallocation support channels.
Funding accommodations are now tracked separately from creator and escape lanes so borrowing-side absorption is visible without being mislabeled as deposit creation.
Holding-company / parent funding remains a cleanup candidate rather than an active interpreted accommodation lane.
The external banking lane now includes broad foreign liabilities plus bank-specific foreign interbank positions and deposits at foreign banks.
The biggest remaining creator-target gap is ci_us_qoq: the live public path still uses all-commercial-bank C&I rather than exact U.S.-addressee domestic nonfinancial C&I.

scope_alignment_context.headline_read: Scope mismatch is real but partial: at h0, removing only the rest-of-world term makes the headline residual about -0.22 less negative, while the true U.S.-chartered bank-leg match makes it about -14.49 less negative.

recommended_release_comparison.headline_read: Keep `total_deposits_bank_qoq` as the headline outcome for now, use `tdc_us_chartered_bank_only_qoq` as the standard scope-check comparison, and keep the no-ROW and deposits-only comparisons as secondary sensitivities.
recommended_release_comparison.rationale: A full headline migration to deposits-only would change the project object more broadly than a scope diagnostic. The cleaner immediate step is to keep the established headline outcome but standardize the true U.S.-chartered bank-leg match as the main scope check, because it isolates bank-leg matching rather than only removing the rest-of-world term.

baseline.description: Imported bank-only headline: Fed + bank-sector + rest-of-world Treasury transactions - Treasury operating cash + positive Fed remittances.

domestic_bank_only.description: No-ROW sensitivity from upstream `tdcest`; removes the rest-of-world Treasury-acquisition term only.

us_chartered_bank_only.description: Local matched-bank-leg sensitivity: Fed Treasury transactions + U.S.-chartered bank Treasury transactions - Treasury operating cash + positive Fed remittances.

takeaways: The `domestic_bank_only` variant removes the rest-of-world Treasury-acquisition term only; it is not a true U.S.-chartered-only bank-leg match.

The `us_chartered_bank_only` variant is the first true bank-leg-matched TDC sensitivity in this repo: Fed Treasury transactions + U.S.-chartered bank Treasury transactions - Treasury operating cash + positive Fed remittances.
The deposits-only concept removes interbank-transactions liabilities from the outcome so the project can distinguish 'deposit scope' from the broader `total_deposits_bank_qoq` object.
At h0 for `total_deposits_including_interbank`, the U.S.-chartered bank-leg-matched TDC makes the derived non-TDC residual more negative than the current baseline by about 14.49.
At h0 for `total_deposits_including_interbank`, removing only the rest-of-world term makes the derived non-TDC residual more negative than the current baseline by about 0.22.
At h0 for `deposits_only_ex_interbank`, the U.S.-chartered bank-leg-matched TDC makes the derived non-TDC residual more negative than the current baseline by about 14.67.
At h0 for `deposits_only_ex_interbank`, removing only the rest-of-world term makes the derived non-TDC residual more negative than the current baseline by about 3.90.

strict_gap_scope_check_context.assumption: Descriptive only: this block holds the baseline strict identifiable total fixed and asks how much the residual-side scope-check shift could mechanically relieve the current strict gap on its own.
strict_gap_scope_check_context.headline_read: The matched-bank-leg scope check only relieves a small share of the current strict gap: at h0, about 1.31 of the baseline strict gap remains when the residual-side scope shift is applied while holding the direct-count strict total fixed.

broad_scope_system_context.headline_read: The broad matched-scope system still leaves a large direct-count gap: at h0, about 0.81 of the broad non-TDC residual remains in the broad strict gap.
takeaways: This artifact compares two matched-scope system reads rather than adding another narrow sensitivity: the current U.S.-chartered matched-bank-leg comparison and a broad-bank matched system built on broad-bank deposits plus a broad loan-led strict core.

The broad matched system keeps the broad headline TDC object and matches it to a broad-bank deposit outcome plus a broad loans-only strict core, rather than trying to force the current U.S.-chartered outcome to absorb the broader treatment.
At h0, the broad matched-scope system leaves about 0.81 of the broad non-TDC residual in the broad strict gap.
Compared with the U.S.-chartered matched-bank-leg read, the broad matched-scope strict gap is smaller at h0 (0.81 versus 1.31).
At h0, the largest treatment-side residual shift in the component audit comes from `no_toc_no_row_bank_only`, with an absolute change of about 43.11.
At h0, removing only ROW changes the headline residual by about -0.22, while removing only foreign bank-sector Treasury legs changes it by about -24.30.

tdc_treatment_audit_context.headline_read: The direct TDC treatment audit identifies `treasury_operating_cash_drain` as the leading signed h0 component; the removal tests now compare Treasury operating cash and ROW directly (26.20 versus -0.22).
takeaways: This audit separates direct baseline-shock responses of the TDC building blocks from residual shifts created by removing whole treatment legs.
Use the direct component responses to see which TDC pieces move with the baseline shock, and use the variant-removal diagnostics to see which omitted legs actually change the residual most.

Quarter-level construction alignment is exact for the public-component reconstruction and all currently audited implied legs, so the remaining issue is dynamic interpretation rather than arithmetic mismatch.
At h0, the largest signed direct component contribution comes from `treasury_operating_cash_drain`.
At h0, removing Treasury operating cash from the treatment shifts the residual by about 26.20.
At h0, removing Treasury operating cash and ROW together shifts the residual by about 43.11.
At h0, removing only ROW shifts the residual by about -0.22, while removing only foreign bank-sector Treasury legs shifts it by about -24.30.
At h0, the combined signed point contribution from foreign bank-sector Treasury legs is about 0.14 in the direct component read.

treasury_operating_cash_audit_context.headline_read: The Treasury-operating-cash audit points to a genuine cash-plumbing pattern rather than a simple sign bug: at h0, TOC ≈ -43.21, TGA ≈ -41.61, reserves ≈ 77.26. Quarter-level TOC/TGA alignment is also tight (corr ≈ 0.95, slope ≈ 0.93; interpretation = `treasury_cash_release_pattern`).
takeaways: This audit checks whether the Treasury-operating-cash leg behaves like genuine TGA plumbing or like a sign/timing/object-definition bug.
Quarter by quarter, the Z.1 Treasury-operating-cash flow and `tga_qoq` move together tightly: correlation ≈ 0.95, TGA-on-TOC slope ≈ 0.93.
At h0, the baseline TDC shock lines up with a Treasury-cash-release pattern: TOC ≈ -43.21, TGA ≈ -41.61, reserves ≈ 77.26.
Because the TDC identity subtracts the Treasury-operating-cash leg, the h0 signed TDC contribution from that leg is about 43.21.

rest_of_world_treasury_audit_context.headline_read: The ROW Treasury-leg audit points away from a simple same-quarter deposit-liability story: at h0, ROW ≈ 15.22, foreign NONTS ≈ 18.61, foreign-bank interbank assets ≈ 4.19. Quarter-level same-quarter alignment is weak (foreign NONTS corr ≈ -0.11, ROW bank-deposit corr ≈ 0.04; interpretation = `external_asset_support_pattern`).
takeaways: This audit checks whether the ROW Treasury leg behaves like a clean external deposit counterpart or a broader external-asset / nontransaction support channel.
Quarter by quarter, the ROW Treasury leg is not a simple same-quarter liability counterpart: corr with `foreign_nonts_qoq` ≈ -0.11, corr with `checkable_rest_of_world_bank_qoq` ≈ 0.04.
At h0, the baseline TDC shock loads positively on the ROW Treasury leg and broader external support variables: ROW ≈ 15.22, foreign NONTS ≈ 18.61, foreign-bank interbank assets ≈ 4.19.
The direct ROW bank-deposit-liability response is comparatively small at h0: checkable ROW deposits ≈ 0.30.

toc_row_path_split_context.headline_read: The TOC/ROW split now separates quarter-level fit from shock-response dominance: quarter by quarter the preferred path is `direct_deposit_path_dominant` (direct corr ≈ 0.86, broad corr ≈ 0.75), but at h0 the preferred path is `broad_support_path_dominant` (broad ≈ 66.05, direct ≈ 42.00).
takeaways: This summary forces a formal split between the TGA-anchored direct-deposit path and the broader support path inside the combined TOC/ROW bundle.
Quarter by quarter, the preferred path is `direct_deposit_path_dominant`: broad-support corr ≈ 0.75, direct-deposit corr ≈ 0.86.
At h0, the preferred path is `broad_support_path_dominant`: broad-support ≈ 66.05, direct-deposit ≈ 42.00.
The broader liquidity-plus-external path remains a larger upper-envelope counterpart at h0, about 101.70.

toc_row_excluded_interpretation_context.headline_read: As a secondary comparison only, excluding TOC/ROW materially changes the h0 residual read: baseline residual ≈ -48.62, TOC/ROW-excluded residual ≈ -5.51. The strict direct-count gap share moves from about 1.06 to about 2.96 under that comparison.
takeaways: This is a secondary comparison surface only: it excludes the combined TOC/ROW bundle from the treatment object to test how much of the current residual and strict-gap story is tied to that suspect block, without relabeling the headline treatment.
At h0, excluding TOC/ROW changes the non-TDC residual response from about -48.62 to about -5.51.
At h0, the strict direct-count gap share moves from about 1.06 under the baseline treatment to about 2.96 under the TOC/ROW-excluded comparison.
At h4, the TOC/ROW-excluded strict gap share is about 1.35, which helps separate treatment-object-definition risk from missing direct-count channels.

strict_missing_channel_context.headline_read: After excluding TOC/ROW as a diagnostic comparison, the strict lane still looks incomplete at h0: residual ≈ -5.50, headline direct core ≈ -10.66, current broad loan source ≈ 5.58, private-borrower-augmented core ≈ 8.49, noncore/system diagnostic ≈ 4.32, securities ≈ 3.48, funding-adjusted net ≈ 9.90. The remaining gap-after-funding share of the residual is about 2.84.
takeaways: This artifact returns to the strict lane after separating the treatment-side TOC/ROW issue: it asks which direct-count pieces still fail to verify the non-TDC residual once the suspect treatment bundle is excluded.
Read the TOC/ROW-excluded block as a diagnostic comparison, not as a replacement headline treatment.
At h0, the strict missing-channel comparison starts from a much smaller residual once TOC/ROW is excluded: baseline residual ≈ -56.69, TOC/ROW-excluded residual ≈ -5.50.

Under the TOC/ROW-excluded comparison at h0, the directly counted pieces still need to be read separately: headline direct core ≈ -10.66, current broad loan source ≈ 5.58, private-borrower-augmented core ≈ 8.49, noncore/system diagnostic ≈ 4.32, securities ≈ 3.48, funding-adjusted net ≈ 9.90.
At h0, the TOC/ROW-excluded comparison exposes a sign mismatch inside the strict lane: the residual stays negative at about -5.50, while the strict identifiable total turns positive at about 10.83.
Even after excluding TOC/ROW, the direct-count verification problem remains large: strict gap share of residual ≈ 2.96, gap-after-funding share ≈ 2.84.
H0 interpretation: `toc_row_exclusion_exposes_sign_mismatch_in_direct_counts`.

strict_sign_mismatch_audit_context.headline_read: The TOC/ROW-excluded shock rotates materially away from the baseline shock: overlap corr ≈ 0.42, same-sign share ≈ 0.72, headline direct-core corr ≈ -0.50 to -0.20, strict identifiable total corr ≈ -0.13 to 0.19. Top five gap quarters explain ≈ 0.33 of absolute gap mass, the largest share is in `covid_post`, and shock-gap corr with the baseline-minus-excluded target bundle is ≈ -0.48.
takeaways: The TOC/ROW-excluded shock is not just a small perturbation of baseline: overlap correlation ≈ 0.42, same-sign share ≈ 0.72.
The excluded shock rotates materially toward positive direct-count channels: headline direct-core corr goes from -0.50 to -0.20, strict identifiable total corr goes from -0.13 to 0.19.
The rotation is concentrated rather than diffuse: the top five shock-gap quarters explain about 0.33 of absolute gap mass, with the largest share in `covid_post`.
The shock-gap lines up with the TOC/ROW target bundle itself: corr with baseline-minus-excluded target ≈ -0.48, ROW leg ≈ -0.44, TOC signed contribution ≈ -0.33.
At h0 this shows up as a sign mismatch in the strict surface: TOC/ROW-excluded residual ≈ -5.50, strict identifiable total ≈ 10.83.

strict_shock_composition_context.headline_read: Shock composition diagnostics show the rotation is led by `covid_post`, but not by only a handful of quarters: dropping the top five gaps gives corr ≈ 0.55, same-sign share ≈ 0.71, while dropping `covid_post` gives corr ≈ 0.57, same-sign share ≈ 0.77.
takeaways: The largest absolute shock-gap share sits in `covid_post`, but the rotation is not limited to a single episode.
Dropping the five largest shock-gap quarters changes the overlap structure to corr ≈ 0.55, same-sign share ≈ 0.71, interpretation = `excluded_shock_only_partially_aligned_with_baseline`.
Dropping the full `covid_post` bucket changes the overlap structure to corr ≈ 0.57, same-sign share ≈ 0.77, interpretation = `excluded_shock_moderately_aligned_but_distinct`.
The largest single quarter right now is `2020Q3` (covid_post), with shock gap ≈ -9.45 and TOC/ROW bundle ≈ 17.90.

strict_top_gap_quarter_audit_context.headline_read: Top-gap quarter diagnostics show the largest gap windows are `mixed` with abs-gap share ≈ 0.42, and the leading contribution pattern is `reinforcing` with abs-gap share ≈ 0.58.
takeaways: Across the top-gap quarters, the largest dominant-leg bucket is `mixed` with abs-gap share ≈ 0.42.
Across the top-gap quarters, the largest contribution pattern is `reinforcing` with abs-gap share ≈ 0.58.
The largest top-gap quarter is `2020Q3` with dominant leg `mixed` and contribution pattern `offsetting`.

strict_top_gap_quarter_direction_context.headline_read: Top-gap quarter direction diagnostics show the leading gap-versus-bundle alignment is `opposed` with abs-gap share ≈ 0.89, and the leading directional-driver bucket is `both_legs_oppose_gap` with abs-gap share ≈ 0.58.
takeaways: Across the top-gap quarters, the leading gap-versus-bundle alignment is `opposed` with abs-gap share ≈ 0.89.
Across the top-gap quarters, the leading directional-driver bucket is `both_legs_oppose_gap` with abs-gap share ≈ 0.58.
The largest top-gap quarter is `2020Q3` with gap-versus-bundle alignment `opposed` and directional driver `toc_driven_gap_direction`.

strict_top_gap_inversion_context.headline_read: Top-gap inversion diagnostics show the leading driver bucket is `both_legs_oppose_gap` with abs-gap share ≈ 0.58; its weighted excluded residual is ≈ 176.91 versus weighted strict total ≈ 347.98.
takeaways: Across the top-gap quarters, the leading inversion bucket is `both_legs_oppose_gap` with abs-gap share ≈ 0.58; its weighted excluded residual is ≈ 176.91 versus weighted strict total ≈ 347.98.
Inside that leading inversion bucket, the largest residual-versus-strict pattern is `positive_residual_positive_strict` with abs-gap share ≈ 0.81.
The TOC-driven exception is `2020Q3`: excluded residual ≈ -217.34, strict total ≈ 89.04, foreign NONTS ≈ -42.43.
The ROW-driven aligned exception is `2009Q2`: excluded residual ≈ -361.40, strict total ≈ -27.00, TGA ≈ 40.59.

strict_top_gap_anomaly_context.headline_read: The main within-bucket anomaly is `2009Q4`: excluded residual ≈ 73.64, strict total ≈ -68.39, anomaly-minus-peer loan delta ≈ -359.67, and anomaly-minus-peer strict-total delta ≈ -513.71.
takeaways: The anomaly quarter is `2009Q4` inside the `both_legs_oppose_gap` bucket, with excluded residual ≈ 73.64 versus strict total ≈ -68.39.

Relative to same-bucket peers, the anomaly quarter differs by loan source ≈ -359.67, securities ≈ -154.03, strict total ≈ -513.71, funding offset ≈ -272.11, foreign NONTS ≈ -331.38, reserves ≈ -469.13.
Among same-bucket peers, the leading residual-versus-strict pattern is `positive_residual_positive_strict` with abs-gap share ≈ 1.00.
The largest anomaly-minus-peer component delta is `strict_identifiable_total_qoq` at ≈ -513.71.

strict_top_gap_anomaly_component_split_c.headline_read: The `2009Q4` anomaly is mainly a `DI loans n.e.c.` shortfall at ≈ -352.49, with `Reserves` also weak at ≈ -469.13.
takeaways: Within the anomaly quarter `2009Q4`, the largest loan-subcomponent gap versus same-bucket peers is `DI loans n.e.c.` at ≈ -352.49.
The main liquidity/external comparison shows `Reserves` at ≈ -469.13; TGA differs by ≈ 263.39.
Across the detailed anomaly blocks, the largest absolute anomaly-minus-peer gap is `reserves_qoq` at ≈ -469.13.

strict_top_gap_anomaly_di_loans_split_co.headline_read: Inside the DI-loans-n.e.c. split, the main borrower-side delta is `Nonfinancial corporate` at ≈ -345.89; borrower-gap delta ≈ 0.00.

di_loans_nec_component_deltas.label: Systemwide DI loans n.e.c. liability total

takeaways: Inside the DI-loans-n.e.c. borrower-side split, the largest named borrower delta is `Nonfinancial corporate` at ≈ -345.89.
The DI-loans-n.e.c. systemwide borrower-gap delta is ≈ 0.00.
This artifact should be read as a borrower-side counterpart diagnostic for the DI-loans-n.e.c. block, not as a same-scope decomposition of the U.S.-chartered lender asset series.

strict_top_gap_anomaly_backdrop_context.headline_read: The `2009Q4` anomaly combines nonfinancial-corporate DI-loans weakness (≈ -345.89) with weaker reserves (≈ -469.13) and weaker foreign NONTS (≈ -331.38).
takeaways: Relative to same-bucket peers, the `2009Q4` anomaly shows nonfinancial-corporate DI loans delta ≈ -345.89, reserves delta ≈ -469.13, and foreign NONTS delta ≈ -331.38.
The TGA delta is ≈ 263.39, while the TOC/ROW-excluded residual delta is ≈ -127.41.
The combined liquidity/external shortfall is about 2.31 times the absolute nonfinancial-corporate DI-loans shortfall.

big_picture_synthesis_context.headline_read: The big-picture synthesis says the residual problem is mostly treatment-side, especially TOC/ROW, but the independent strict lane still does not validate the remaining object: after excluding TOC/ROW at h0, residual ≈ -5.51 while strict identifiable total ≈ 10.83.
takeaways: Scope mismatch is real but partial: at h0 the true U.S.-chartered bank-leg match shifts the residual by about -14.49, versus about -0.22 for the no-ROW sensitivity.
Even after broadening to a matched-scope bank system, the strict lane still leaves a large gap: about 0.81 of the broad residual remains in the broad strict gap at h0.
The residual problem is concentrated in TOC/ROW, not foreign bank sectors: baseline h0 residual ≈ -48.62, removing TOC shifts it by about 26.20, and removing TOC+ROW shifts it by about 43.11.
But TOC/ROW exclusion does not validate the independent lane: the h0 residual shrinks to about -5.51 while the strict identifiable total is about 10.83, with strict-gap share about 2.96.
Funding adjustment does not rescue the strict lane under that comparison: funding-adjusted net is about 9.90 and the remaining gap-after-funding share is about 2.84.

The TOC/ROW-excluded shock is not a stable replacement object: overlap correlation with baseline is about 0.42, same-sign share about 0.72, and the top five gap quarters explain about 0.33 of gap mass.
That instability is concentrated in `covid_post`, so the excluded shock rotates around specific windows rather than delivering a clean new baseline.

The quarter-level anomaly work is now enough for classification and should stay secondary: in `2009Q4`, the combined liquidity/external shortfall is about 2.31 times the corporate-credit shortfall (anomaly_combines_corporate_credit_shortfall_with_even_larger_liquidity_external_shortfall).

treatment_object_comparison_context.headline_read: Treatment-object comparison recommends `split_core_plus_support_bundle` and says to keep current headline provisional and do not promote the TOC_ROW_excluded object.

recommendation.headline_decision_now: keep current headline provisional and do not promote the TOC_ROW_excluded object
recommendation.why: Baseline full TDC still best represents the broad original concept, but the residual problem is concentrated in TOC/ROW. The TOC/ROW-excluded object is useful diagnostically yet too unstable to replace the headline. So the next serious branch should redesign the treatment architecture into a deposit-proximate core plus an explicit TOC/ROW support bundle.

candidate_objects.object_definition: Fed + bank-sector + ROW Treasury transactions - TOC + positive Fed remittances
candidate_objects.why_it_exists: Conceptually closest to the original full Treasury-attributed deposit object.
candidate_objects.scope_alignment_note: Broader than the U.S.-chartered headline outcome; scope mismatch is real but not dominant.
candidate_objects.treatment_object_risk: TOC and ROW appear to be a mixed support bundle rather than a clean same-object deposit-treatment leg.

candidate_objects.object_definition: Bank-leg-matched TDC sensitivity on the U.S.-chartered outcome scope.
candidate_objects.why_it_exists: Best scope-diagnostic comparison against the current headline outcome.
candidate_objects.scope_alignment_note: Improves scope coherence but does not solve the main residual problem.
candidate_objects.treatment_object_risk: Too narrow to stand in for the full TDC object if ROW is conceptually part of TDC.

candidate_objects.object_definition: Baseline TDC with TOC and ROW removed together.
candidate_objects.why_it_exists: Tests how much of the residual problem is concentrated in the TOC/ROW block.
candidate_objects.scope_alignment_note: Useful diagnostic, not a stable replacement treatment.
candidate_objects.treatment_object_risk: Shock rotates materially and the independent lane still does not validate the remaining object.

candidate_objects.object_definition: Publish a deposit-proximate core TDC separately from a TOC/ROW support bundle instead of forcing them into one headline object.
candidate_objects.why_it_exists: Current evidence says TOC/ROW dominates the treatment-side problem, while a TOC/ROW-excluded object is not stable enough to become the new headline shock.
candidate_objects.scope_alignment_note: Preserves the broad TDC idea while separating the mixed support bundle from the deposit-proximate core.
candidate_objects.treatment_object_risk: Requires an explicit redesign of the headline interpretation layer rather than a simple variant swap.

takeaways: Baseline full TDC remains the broad conceptual object, but its h0 residual is about -48.62 and the main treatment-side movement comes from TOC/ROW rather than foreign bank sectors.
The U.S.-chartered bank-leg match is the right scope comparison, but it only shifts the h0 residual by about -14.49.
The TOC/ROW-excluded object is diagnostic only: h0 residual ≈ -5.51, strict total ≈ 10.83, overlap corr with baseline ≈ 0.42.
The broad matched-scope system still leaves a large h0 strict gap share (≈ 0.81), so the project does not currently have a clean validated replacement treatment object.

The most defensible next step is a split treatment architecture: keep the broad TDC concept visible, but separate the TOC/ROW support bundle from the deposit-proximate core instead of forcing one headline object to do both jobs.

split_treatment_architecture_context.headline_read: Split treatment architecture now makes the redesign explicit: h0 support bundle ≈ 23.31, core residual ≈ -5.51, and the repo recommendation is `split_core_plus_support_bundle`.

architecture_recommendation.headline_decision_now: keep current headline provisional and do not promote the TOC_ROW_excluded object

takeaways: The split architecture defines a deposit-proximate core treatment separately from the mixed TOC/ROW support bundle instead of forcing one headline treatment object to do both jobs.
The quarterly split is mechanically exact in the panel: max TDC decomposition gap ≈ 4.26e-14, max residual decomposition gap ≈ 1.14e-13.
At h0, baseline residual ≈ -48.62, core residual ≈ -5.51, so the split support bundle carries about 23.31 of treatment-side movement.
At h0, the deposit-proximate core treatment response is still sizable (≈ 63.10), which is why the split should be treated as an interpretation redesign rather than a zeroing-out exercise.
Path evidence stays mixed inside the support bundle: quarter by quarter the preferred path is `direct_deposit_path_dominant`, but at h0 the preferred path is `broad_support_path_dominant`.
This split directly implements the repo-level redesign recommendation: `split_core_plus_support_bundle`.

core_treatment_promotion_context.headline_read: Core-treatment promotion diagnostics keep the split interpretive for now: baseline/core shock corr ≈ 0.42, same-sign share ≈ 0.72, h0 core residual ≈ -5.51, strict total ≈ 10.83.

promotion_recommendation.why: The core deposit-proximate series is currently a semantic alias of the no-TOC/no-ROW treatment, and the separately estimated core shock should stay interpretive until it delivers a clearer validated object than the existing diagnostic branch.
promotion_recommendation.current_release_role: keep split architecture interpretive and diagnostic

takeaways: The current core deposit-proximate target is mechanically the same series as the legacy no-TOC/no-ROW diagnostic, so promoting it is a semantic and reporting decision, not a new target-data discovery.
Baseline and core shocks overlap only moderately (corr ≈ 0.42, same-sign share ≈ 0.72), so a promoted core shock would not just be a trivial relabeling of the frozen baseline shock.
Under separate estimation, the core treatment still has a live h0 target response (≈ 63.10) and a small remaining residual (≈ -5.51).
But the direct-count validation is still not there at h0: core residual ≈ -5.51, strict identifiable total ≈ 10.83, sign_match = false.

The core deposit-proximate series is currently a semantic alias of the no-TOC/no-ROW treatment, and the separately estimated core shock should stay interpretive until it delivers a clearer validated object than the existing diagnostic branch.

strict_redesign_context.headline_read: Strict redesign should now focus on loan composition, not more treatment relabeling: matched-bank-leg scope still leaves about 1.31 of the baseline strict gap, h0 core residual is about -5.51 while the direct-count total is about 10.83, the dominant h0 loan block is `strict_loan_consumer_credit_qoq`, and funding offsets are about 1.53 of identifiable total.

recommended_build_order.why: The treatment split is now explicit but the direct-count failure remains: the h0 core residual is still about -5.51 while the strict identifiable total is about 10.83.
recommended_build_order.deliverable: Create a release-facing core-loan subtotal that keeps consumer credit and mortgages explicit and moves broad DI-loans-n.e.c. content out of the headline core until it is subdivided.

recommended_build_order.why: At h0 the dominant loan block is `strict_loan_consumer_credit_qoq` and DI-loans-n.e.c. contributes only about 0.20 of signed loan-source beta, so the next problem is not simply “add more DI loans n.e.c.”; it is classifying broad system/financial/external credit separately from deposit-proximate core credit.
recommended_build_order.deliverable: Publish a DI-loans-n.e.c. split that separates private-credit-like content from system, financial, interbank, and external content using the existing borrower-side diagnostics as scaffolding.

recommended_build_order.why: Funding offsets are already about 1.53 of the signed strict identifiable total at h0, so broadening the headline strict lane with more netting now would blur the failure rather than clarify it.
recommended_build_order.deliverable: Leave securities and funding-offset blocks as sensitivities while the loan-core redesign is evaluated against the split treatment architecture.

takeaways: The treatment-side redesign is now stable enough to stop debating the headline split and return to the strict lane itself.
At h0, the split treatment leaves a large support bundle (≈ 23.31) but a much smaller core residual (≈ -5.51).
The matched-bank-leg scope check is no longer the main strict-lane issue: about 1.31 of the baseline strict gap still remains when only that scope shift is applied.
Even on the TOC/ROW-excluded comparison, the direct-count strict lane still points the wrong way at h0: residual ≈ -5.50, strict identifiable total ≈ 10.83.

The h0 loan picture does not justify treating DI-loans-n.e.c. as the headline core: the dominant loan block is `strict_loan_consumer_credit_qoq` and DI-loans-n.e.c. contributes only about 0.20 of signed loan-source beta.
Funding offsets are already material at h0 (about 1.53 of signed identifiable total), so the next strict branch should stabilize the loan core before revisiting more netting.
The separate core-treatment shock stays interpretive only for now, so strict-lane redesign should proceed under the current split architecture rather than around a newly promoted treatment.

strict_loan_core_redesign_context.headline_read: The strict loan-core redesign is now concrete under the core-deposit-proximate shock: core residual ≈ -5.50, current broad loan source ≈ 5.58, direct minimum core ≈ -10.66, private-borrower-augmented core ≈ 8.49, noncore/system diagnostic ≈ 4.32. Published role design: headline direct core = `strict_loan_core_min_qoq`, standard secondary comparison = `strict_loan_core_plus_private_borrower_qoq`, and the old broad loan subtotal plus DI-loans-n.e.c. remain diagnostic only.

headline_direct_core.definition: Conservative same-scope direct core: mortgages plus consumer credit only.
headline_direct_core.why: Best current same-scope direct-count candidate after removing the unresolved DI-loans-n.e.c. bucket from the headline core.

standard_secondary_comparison.definition: Headline direct core plus the private-domestic borrower slice of DI loans n.e.c.
standard_secondary_comparison.why: Useful bounded comparison for how much private-borrower DI activity changes the headline read without promoting the full DI bucket into the core.

broad_loan_subtotal_diagnostic.definition: Historical broad loan subtotal including DI loans n.e.c. and other advances.
broad_loan_subtotal_diagnostic.why: Still informative as a subtotal, but no longer suitable as the headline direct core because it embeds the unresolved DI-loans-n.e.c. classification problem.

di_bucket_diagnostic.definition: Broad DI-loans-n.e.c. bucket prior to a settled core-vs-noncore split.
di_bucket_diagnostic.why: Should be interpreted through borrower and noncore/system diagnostics, not as a headline-capable direct core component.

noncore_system_diagnostic.definition: State/local, domestic-financial, ROW, borrower-gap, and other-advances subtotal.
noncore_system_diagnostic.why: Tracks the system/noncore portion of the old broad loan subtotal and helps keep the redesigned headline core narrow.

takeaways: This artifact turns the strict-redesign plan into a concrete loan-core comparison under both the baseline and core-deposit-proximate shocks.

The redesigned direct minimum core is intentionally conservative: mortgages plus consumer credit only, with the broad DI-loans-n.e.c. bucket removed from the headline direct core until it is subdivided.

At h0, the redesigned direct minimum core should be read as the safer headline direct core because it keeps same-scope direct measurement explicit while the broad current loan source still embeds the unresolved DI-loans-n.e.c. bucket (direct-core abs gap to core residual ≈ 5.16, current broad-loan abs gap ≈ 11.08).

The published role design should now be explicit: `strict_loan_core_min_qoq` is the headline direct core, `strict_loan_core_plus_private_borrower_qoq` is the standard secondary comparison, and both `strict_loan_source_qoq` and `strict_loan_di_loans_nec_qoq` remain diagnostic-only until the DI bucket is redesigned more fully.

takeaways: This surface fixes the published role of the broad DI-loans-n.e.c. bucket inside the redesigned strict loan lane.

strict_corporate_bridge_secondary_compar.headline_read: The secondary strict comparison is now estimated directly at h0 under the core-deposit-proximate shock: core residual ≈ -5.50, headline direct core ≈ -10.66, core + private bridge ≈ 8.49, core + nonfinancial corporate ≈ 9.44.
takeaways: This surface asks whether the standard secondary strict comparison should remain the broad private bridge or narrow to the nonfinancial-corporate bridge.
It keeps the headline direct core fixed and compares only the secondary role candidates against the same residual target.

Absolute h0 gaps to the core residual are now explicit: direct core ≈ 5.16, core + private bridge ≈ 13.99, core + nonfinancial corporate ≈ 14.94.

strict_component_framework_context.headline_read: The strict deposit-component framework is now frozen in one place: h0 TOC/ROW support bundle ≈ 23.31, core residual ≈ -5.51, headline direct core ≈ -10.66, standard bridge comparison ≈ 9.44, wider diagnostic envelope ≈ 8.49.
takeaways: Closure-oriented accounting remains out of the independent-evidence tier; it can still be useful descriptively, but it is frozen as non-evidence for strict deposit-component verification.
The current full TDC stays in the repo as the provisional broad Treasury-attributed object, not as a settled strict deposit component.
TOC/ROW is now frozen as a real measured Treasury support bundle with unresolved strict deposit incidence, not just as vague specification risk.
The headline strict direct evidence is frozen as `strict_loan_core_min_qoq`.

The standard bridge comparison is frozen as `strict_loan_core_plus_nonfinancial_corporate_qoq`, while `strict_loan_core_plus_private_borrower_qoq` becomes the wider diagnostic envelope and the private-offset block stays `diagnostic_only`.
The split-treatment read stays central to the framing: h0 TOC/ROW support bundle ≈ 23.31, core residual ≈ -5.51.

The strict comparison roles are frozen off the direct h0 comparison under the core-deposit-proximate shock: headline direct core ≈ -10.66, standard bridge comparison ≈ 9.44, wider diagnostic envelope ≈ 8.49.

The role assignment is no longer based on raw h0 fit alone: fit-preferred bridge = `strict_loan_core_plus_private_borrower_qoq`, but the frozen strict-design bridge stays `strict_loan_core_plus_nonfinancial_corporate_qoq`.
The direct-core headline is now explicitly horizon-aware: multihorizon benchmark = `strict_loan_core_min_qoq`, impact-horizon candidate = `strict_loan_mortgages_qoq`.
Current horizon winners = {'h0': 'strict_loan_mortgages_qoq', 'h4': 'strict_loan_core_min_qoq', 'h8': 'strict_loan_core_min_qoq'}.
The deposit-proximate core shock remains `keep_interpretive_only`, so the split architecture is still interpretive rather than promoted as the new headline treatment.
The raw-units TOC/ROW liability-incidence audit is now the main decision gate: decision = `full_reincorporation_not_supported`.
The narrow-TOC candidate gate is now settled too: decision = `keep_toc_outside_strict_object_under_current_evidence`.

toc_row_incidence_audit_context.headline_read: The TOC/ROW incidence audit now makes the strict-incidence problem concrete at h0: TOC deposit-proxy share ≈ 0.82 versus reserve share ≈ 1.79, ROW deposit-proxy share ≈ 0.02 versus external-support share ≈ 1.22.
takeaways: This is the first-pass liability-incidence audit for the TOC/ROW block. It treats the bundle as economically real and asks whether each leg behaves like an in-scope bank-deposit component rather than only like broad support.
The goal here is not to relitigate arithmetic. It is to test strict deposit incidence by leg using the already-built TOC, ROW, and path-split evidence.
TOC continues to look like real Treasury cash plumbing in raw quarterly data: contemporaneous TGA-vs-TOC corr ≈ 0.95.
But TOC does not yet earn strict-component status: at h0 the in-scope deposit proxy captures only about 0.82 of TOC while reserves capture about 1.79.
ROW looks weaker as an in-scope deposit leg and stronger as broader external support: checkable-ROW-bank corr ≈ 0.04, foreign-NONTS corr ≈ -0.11.
The h0 ROW split points the same way: in-scope deposit proxy share ≈ 0.02, external-support share ≈ 1.22.

The combined support bundle remains the dominant treatment-side issue: h0 TOC/ROW support bundle ≈ 23.31, with TOC signed contribution ≈ 43.21, ROW signed contribution ≈ 15.22, while the remaining core residual is only ≈ -5.51.
The existing path split still matters for interpretation: quarterly preferred bundle path = `direct_deposit_path_dominant`, but the bundle is not yet validated as a strict bank-deposit component.

toc_row_liability_incidence_raw_context.headline_read: The raw-units TOC/ROW liability-incidence audit sharpens the binary gate at h0: TOC deposits-only share ≈ 0.68 versus reserves share ≈ 1.79, ROW-checkable share ≈ 0.02 versus foreign-NONTS share ≈ 1.22.
takeaways: This is the raw-units liability-incidence audit for the TOC and ROW legs. It asks whether each measured leg lands in the in-scope bank-deposit aggregate strongly enough to count toward a strict deposit component.
The audit is scope matched to the bank-deposit outcomes and timing matched across same-quarter and next-quarter raw mappings, with h0/h1 shock overlays kept separate.
TOC still looks only partially deposit-incidence-valid in raw units: best in-scope deposit corr ≈ 0.25, best support corr ≈ 0.95.
The h0 TOC overlay points the same way: deposits-only share ≈ 0.68, total-deposits share ≈ 0.87, reserves share ≈ 1.79.
ROW looks weaker as a strict deposit leg in raw units: best in-scope deposit corr ≈ 0.10, best support/external corr ≈ 0.12.
The h0 ROW overlay is even less supportive of strict incidence: deposits-only share ≈ 1.93, ROW-checkable share ≈ 0.02, foreign-NONTS share ≈ 1.22.
Current binary-gate verdict: do not reincorporate the full TOC/ROW block into the strict object. At most, TOC remains a candidate for a narrower validated share in a later branch.

toc_validated_share_candidate_context.headline_read: The narrow-TOC candidate gate now closes that branch directly: core residual ≈ -5.51, direct core ≈ -10.66, best TOC-share candidate implied residual ≈ -22.85 with abs gap ≈ 12.19.
takeaways: This summary tests the only remaining reincorporation question after the raw-incidence gate: whether any narrow TOC share can be added back to the strict object without violating the strict standard.
The rule is strict, not fit-chasing: a candidate must both clear the quarterly incidence gate and improve the direct-core comparison relative to the current core residual.
The baseline comparison benchmark is the current core residual versus the headline direct strict core: core residual ≈ -5.51, direct core ≈ -10.66, abs gap ≈ 5.15.
Even the best narrow TOC candidate does not help under the current evidence: `h0_private_checkable_share_candidate` implies residual ≈ -22.85 with abs gap to the direct core ≈ 12.19.
The quarterly gate still fails for TOC incidence stability: best in-scope deposit corr ≈ 0.25 versus best support corr ≈ 0.95.
Current decision: keep TOC outside the strict object alongside ROW. Under the present evidence, TOC remains a real measured support leg, not a validated strict deposit component.

strict_release_framing_context.headline_read: The strict release framing is now frozen explicitly: TOC/ROW support bundle ≈ 23.31, core residual ≈ -5.51, headline direct core ≈ -10.66, TOC deposits-only share ≈ 0.68, ROW-checkable share ≈ 0.02; TOC and ROW stay outside the strict object under current evidence.

strict_direct_core_component_context.headline_read: The strict direct-core component split is now explicit at h0 under the core-deposit-proximate shock: residual ≈ -5.50, mortgages ≈ -5.04, consumer credit ≈ -4.35, bundled direct core ≈ -10.66.
takeaways: This summary asks whether the current headline strict direct core should stay bundled or narrow further inside the current non-TOC/ROW framework.
The comparison is intentionally narrow: mortgages only, consumer credit only, and the current bundled direct core, all judged against the same residual object.

The dominant direct-core subcomponent at h0 is `strict_loan_mortgages_qoq`.
Current recommendation: `mortgages_only_candidate_deserves_review`.

strict_direct_core_horizon_stability_con.headline_read: The direct-core winner is now horizon-specific rather than universal: h0 = `strict_loan_mortgages_qoq`, h4 = `strict_loan_core_min_qoq`, h8 = `strict_loan_core_min_qoq`.
takeaways: This summary asks whether the direct-core winner is stable across horizons or only an impact-horizon result.
Horizon-by-horizon winners = {'h0': 'strict_loan_mortgages_qoq', 'h4': 'strict_loan_core_min_qoq', 'h8': 'strict_loan_core_min_qoq'}.
Mortgages are the impact-horizon winner, but the bundled core retakes the closest-to-residual slot by h4 and h8, so the current bundled direct core should stay the multihorizon benchmark for now.

strict_additional_creator_candidate_cont.headline_read: The additional-creator candidate search is now explicit at h0 under the core-deposit-proximate shock: best broad validation proxy = `closed_end_residential_loans_qoq` (beta ≈ -2.56), best extension candidate = `cre_multifamily_loans_qoq` (beta ≈ -0.29).
takeaways: This surface asks a narrower question than the earlier bridge work: are there any additional mechanical creator candidates beyond the current direct core, or only broader validation proxies?

Validation proxies are broad all-bank residential and consumer loan families that overlap the current direct core conceptually; extension candidates are business, CRE, and clearly noncore loan families outside the current direct core.
At h0 under the core-deposit-proximate shock, the closest broad validation proxy is `closed_end_residential_loans_qoq` with beta ≈ -2.56 against residual ≈ -5.50.
The closest additional extension candidate at h0 is `cre_multifamily_loans_qoq` with beta ≈ -0.29; this is the best remaining non-core creator candidate, not a promoted strict channel.

The impact-horizon direct-core candidate remains `strict_loan_mortgages_qoq`, so broad validation proxies should be read mainly as cross-checks on that existing channel rather than as new strict components.
Current recommendation: `no_additional_extension_candidate_supported`.

strict_di_loans_nec_measurement_audit_co.headline_read: The DI-loans-n.e.c. measurement audit now says the public data still do not isolate a promotable same-scope transaction split: DI aggregate ≈ 17.58, best cross-scope bridge = `strict_di_loans_nec_nonfinancial_corporate_qoq` at ≈ 20.82, best same-scope proxy = `loans_to_nondepository_financial_institutions_qoq` at ≈ 5.13.
takeaways: This audit asks a narrower measurement question than the bridge surfaces: can the broad DI-loans-n.e.c. lender transaction row be split into any clean same-scope transaction-based subcomponent using current public data?

The answer is constrained by the current data map itself: the repo has one direct lender-side transaction row for the DI bucket, a cross-scope borrower-counterpart family, and only proxy-level same-scope slices for likely noncore pieces.
At h0 under the core-deposit-proximate shock, the closest cross-scope transaction bridge is `strict_di_loans_nec_nonfinancial_corporate_qoq` with beta ≈ 20.82 against DI aggregate ≈ 17.58.

The closest same-scope proxy slice is `loans_to_nondepository_financial_institutions_qoq` with beta ≈ 5.13, but it remains a proxy-level level-change family rather than a clean lender-side transaction split.

So the current public data do not support promoting any DI-loans-n.e.c. subcomponent into the strict object: the exact same-scope transaction subcomponent is unavailable, the best bridges are cross-scope, and the best same-scope slices are proxy-only.

strict_results_closeout_context.headline_read: The strict closeout summary now freezes the end-state directly: core residual ≈ -5.51, headline direct core ≈ -10.66, standard bridge comparison ≈ 9.44.
settled_findings: Full TDC remains the broad Treasury-attributed object; it is not currently validated as the strict deposit component.
The strict object excludes both TOC and ROW under current evidence.
The multihorizon direct strict benchmark is `strict_loan_core_min_qoq`.
The standard narrow bridge comparison is `strict_loan_core_plus_nonfinancial_corporate_qoq`.
Closure-style accounting is descriptive only and does not count as independent verification.
The additional creator-channel search is closed under current evidence: `no_additional_extension_candidate_supported`.
The final DI-loans-n.e.c. audit is also closed under current public data: `no_promotable_same_scope_transaction_subcomponent_supported`.

Whether the strict lane is still missing major direct creator channels beyond the current loan core.

unresolved_questions: Whether any future scope- and timing-matched incidence evidence validates a narrow in-scope TOC share.

Whether any genuinely new same-scope mechanical deposit-creation channel appears beyond the current direct core.
takeaways: This is the final closeout surface for the strict branch: it summarizes what is settled, what remains unresolved, and whether further empirical expansion is still justified.
The repo now has a frozen broad-vs-strict split rather than an open-ended TDC redesign loop.
The strict side is intentionally narrow: direct core first, bridge second, support bundle outside.
Current h0 snapshot: TOC/ROW support bundle ≈ 23.31, core residual ≈ -5.51, headline direct core ≈ -10.66, standard bridge comparison ≈ 9.44.

The unresolved DI bucket still does not yield a promotable same-scope transaction split: best cross-scope bridge = `strict_di_loans_nec_nonfinancial_corporate_qoq`, best same-scope proxy = `loans_to_nondepository_financial_institutions_qoq`.
The remaining creator-channel search is also exhausted for now: best extension candidate = `cre_multifamily_loans_qoq`, but no additional extension candidate is supported.

series_roles.description: Canonical broad bank-only TDC headline imported from tdcest.
Interest-cleaned broad bank-only comparison from the tdcest Tier 2 ladder.
Fiscal-corrected broad bank-only comparison from the tdcest Tier 3 ladder.
Fiscal-corrected broad-depository comparison from the tdcest Tier 3 ladder.
Historical bank-receipt overlay candidate from tdcest's age-eligible window.
Bounded MRV/CBSP ROW receipt pilot imported as a nondefault sensitivity only.

takeaways: tdcpass already uses the canonical tdcest broad headline; this integration adds the richer corrected ladder and bounded downstream surfaces rather than replacing the strict framework.
Tier 2 and Tier 3 are comparison rows for the broad object, not new strict deposit-component defaults.
The historical bank-receipt overlay is useful historical-only context, and the MRV ROW branch remains bounded nondefault sensitivity only.
Latest corrected broad-bank read: Tier 2 ≈ -49.49, Tier 3 ≈ -51.57.
Historical overlay is present and should stay fenced to the historical-age window: latest nonzero quarter = 2024Q4.
The ROW MRV pilot is now directly importable, but it remains explicitly nondefault: latest nonzero quarter = 2025Q3.

tdcest_broad_object_comparison_context.headline_read: Latest common broad-object comparison 2025Q4: headline ≈ 69.00, Tier 2 ≈ -49.49, Tier 3 ≈ -51.57, Tier 3 broad-depository ≈ -51.35; historical overlay latest nonzero quarter = 2024Q4, ROW MRV pilot latest nonzero quarter = 2025Q3.
takeaways: The sibling tdcest ladder is now fully visible inside tdcpass as broad-object comparison rows rather than a replacement strict framework.
Tier 2 and Tier 3 stay in the broad-object comparison tier; they are not promoted into the strict deposit-component object.
The historical bank-receipt overlay and the MRV ROW pilot are now live in the quarterly panel, but they remain fenced to historical-only and nondefault-sensitivity roles.
Latest common broad-object quarter 2025Q4: headline ≈ 69.00, Tier 2 ≈ -49.49, Tier 3 ≈ -51.57, Tier 3 broad-depository ≈ -51.35.
Historical overlay remains active only in a narrow historical window: latest nonzero quarter = 2024Q4.
The bounded ROW MRV pilot is live but explicitly nondefault: latest nonzero quarter = 2025Q3.

tdcest_broad_treatment_sensitivity_conte.headline_read: The corrected tdcest broad-treatment LP sensitivity layer is blocked by short history under the frozen shock-design gate. On a separate short-history exploratory h0 check, the common-sample baseline is total ≈ 79.48, residual ≈ -122.68, while Tier 2 is total ≈ 73.26, residual ≈ -129.14.

recommendation.why: The corrected tdcest broad-treatment variants currently add value as broad-object level/ladder diagnostics, but there is not enough history to run the frozen LP sensitivity design on them.

takeaways: Baseline h0 broad-object read remains total deposits up and other component down: total ≈ 28.77, residual ≈ -56.69.
Corrected tdcest broad-treatment LP sensitivity is not currently estimable under the frozen shock-design gate because the corrected series do not have enough history to enter the sensitivity ladder.

Current availability by corrected variant: Tier 3 fiscal-corrected bank-only: target non-null count = 16, usable shock count = 0; Tier 3 fiscal-corrected broad-depository: target non-null count = 16, usable shock count = 0.
A separate short-history exploratory h0 check is available, but it is not comparable to the frozen rolling-shock LP design.
Short-history common-sample baseline h0 read is total ≈ 79.48, residual ≈ -122.68 over 2022Q1 to 2025Q4.

Short-history corrected-variant h0 reads: Tier 2 interest-corrected bank-only: total ≈ 73.26, residual ≈ -129.14; Tier 3 fiscal-corrected bank-only: total ≈ 72.81, residual ≈ -129.46; Tier 3 fiscal-corrected broad-depository: total ≈ 72.32, residual ≈ -130.13.

readiness_reasons: Structural proxy cross-checks do not yet corroborate the non-TDC residual at key horizons.
The structural proxy bundle still leaves a large uncovered remainder at key horizons.
readiness_warnings: 7 unexpected-TDC shock windows are scale-ratio flagged (1 severe tail and 6 milder threshold breaches), but the published sample-sensitivity trims do not overturn the headline sign pattern.
The exact identity baseline is primary, but the secondary approximate dynamic path still diverges materially at key horizons.
